label_values.value:
It's the change of circumstances not the mind. Whatever is happening in bengal from grassroot level party worker to mla and MP shows the lust for power (satta) in all and sundry. It is this powerlust at such a widespread level that makes bengal different from most/all other states. In no other state is politics a source or employment at such a large scale (from local people to tollywood to cricketers to painters to artists to almost everyone resorts to politics as a means of earning and employment even the most successful icon of this century from Bengal could not resist it) 
It will be interesting to see if BJP yields to the local trend or tries to change it

label_values.value:
Sir

As u always say facts are sacred so let's stick to pure facts

1. BJP got 45.84 pc votes
2. Tmc got 40.8
3. In absolute terms bjp got 32 lacs votes more than tmc
4. In 2021 599 lacs votes were cast in 2026 631 lacs which is 32 lacs more were cast
5. If just 16 lacs votes had moved from bjp to tmc the results could have been totally different and similar to Kolkata wherein spite of such a one sided election - Chowringhee, Balygunj, Entally Beleghata all voted for TMC
6. If we take Howrah and Kolkata combined TMC has won more seats than BJP in what is otherwise a totally one sided election and if rest of Bengal followed their path TMC would still be in power. In Kolkata + Howrah region tmc got 14 seats and BJP 13 

And see the quantum of actions that had to be taken to bring votes of Bengal out of their stupor (SIR, Para Military forces, BJP getting its own house in order, Amit Shah sitting in bengal for 15 days, managing SC from the clutches of Sighvi and Sibal, TMC own hubris and share of mistakes (ekta community jokhon, makka madina, 4 may dupur 12ta DJ, folding up of IPAC, financially constraining TMC by using ED/CBI/Income tax and my list has 105 such action points) 

So while we all are extremely happy that we got rid of the devil/evil and now that facts of their wrongdoings are pouring out everyday lets not forget that 
1. even in a free and fair election with such a large participation 40.8% still voted for TMC 
2. in Kolkata + Howrah TMC got more seats than BJP 
3. some of the elite seats of Kolkata - Ballygunj, Chowringhee still voted for TMC in large nos, Jadavpur saved the day.

Panchali Singh Anand this happens when we form an opinion even without watching anyway sharing the story and the video

The movie Dhurandhar does not feature the real P. Chidambaram as a character, but it contains a storyline that alludes to real-life allegations involving a former Indian Finance Minister and a fake currency scam. 
The film depicts a scenario where an Indian minister and his son allegedly help sell sensitive national assets, specifically currency printing plates or high-quality currency paper, to Pakistan's intelligence agency ISI, which then uses them to infiltrate India with high-quality fake currency to destabilize the economy. 
This plot point is widely interpreted as a reference to real-world controversies and allegations that surfaced during the Congress-led UPA government's tenure when P. Chidambaram served as the Finance Minister.

https://www.instagram.com/reel/DSIQN3Oj3x5/

Collect all the facts this movie has uncovered (most of the characters and events have real life connection) and then may be re-watch it u will understand why the entire leftist liberal pro pak literati in India is so rattled with this movie.

Good luck

Sumit Duttau got me wrong completely i didn't even mention yunus or sen babu's faith in him, this widely prevalent sense of bengali identity is a root cause of many problems.

Any set of oeople who have a sense of identity higher than their national identity be it

Political - communists
Religious - we all know whoM
Regional - bengal, punjab

label_values.value:
Ramayan and mahabharat are two epics which perhaps Hv the greatest influence on  our country our lives our culture. In Ramayan thr battle was fought following the dharma and hence no rules were broken but mahabharat was opposite every rule was broken but Krishna ensured that Pandav are not the first one to break the rules.

In our life also we come across battles where dharma is followed and where rules (dharma) are not being followed. We accordingly hv to decide our strategy. The battle of adharma cannot be fought with dharma that's the key msg of Krishna when he asks Arjun to kill unarmed Karan and when Karan questions Krishna He reminds him off everything from what he called Draupadi in Rajyasabha to the immoral killing of abhimanyu of which karan was a part.

In summary let's try and fool ourselves into believing that when one side across globe is fighting the battle with all sorts of adharma the other side can safeguard itself with dosti and aman ki asha. The battle of adharma cannot be fought with dharma. The sooner as a society we understand this better for all of us. And let me add to this that - de dee hamein azadi bina khada g bina dhaal - is the biggest lie we hv been made to believe in and with passage of time this shall will get uncovered to more and more people. It was the navy mutiny of 1946 at Bombay (also called the last battle of independence) which got us independence and not the Quit India movement which happened before the WW2 in 1942 and lost all its relevance after WW2.

There is much more that can be written but in interest of brevity I must pause

Sir

My humble submission as below, not with a view to contest or contradict but just to add another perspective. 

If one side is resorting to adharma the other side cannot fight the battle with dharma. This is the crux of mahabharat. Bhishma, Drona, karna, duryodhan were all killed unethically and planned by non other than lord krishna himself. Karna asked Krishna is it dharma to kill an unarmed person in the battle to which Krishna replied where was yr dharma when draupadi was being disrobed and abhimanyu was being killer. 

In this battle full of corrupt people on both sides the only choice people will get is too chose the less or least corrupt of them all and also live with the fact each one (even kejriwal) will call themself an epitome of virtue and honesty.

As long as there are political parties who hardly have any legitimate means of earning and enormous expenses and with all major donations coming only as quid pro quo corruption seems inevitable.

Got reminded of this note I had written the day after I got fractured some months ago

Time is not a resource which needs to be managed nor a commodity which needs to be used. 

Time is life itself and life is valuable because it is limited. How you spend yr time is how u live yr life. 

And currently most if not all of us are rushing thru our lives trying to pack more and more action into less and less time and this quest is endless. We are even teaching our children to do the same. Technology which was supposed to an enabler for saving time has been converted into a tool for achieving more in less time rather than enjoying the time it saves. 

This has also to do with our economic model which is based on unlimited growth. For the world to survive it has to keep growing economically and the responsibility for this growth has been subtly passed on to people are economically well off.

Its time to rethink the concept of time, growth, to really think if less is more, to reflect upon if gratification is happiness. I don't know what the correct answer is but I know for sure that the model most people are following currently needs a serious revisit.

Take time to live for u never know when it's time to leave

I feel the issue is more to do with wrong history that has been purposely fed to us and which needs correction. No other country in the world eulogies their occupiers with their title great. Our glorious past has been severely undermined and the foreign forces have been unduly glorified. Even the war of independence has been attributed to just two persons and everyone else has been underplayed. I will quote just 1 example - quit India movement which happened in 1942 has been given undue credit since there was entire world war btwn British leaving India and the QI movement. The naval mutiny of 1946 at bombay which was the real trigger behind British quitting India has been ignored and today even well informed persons are not aware of this historic event. The history taught to us is full of such errors and it is this errro that needs correction but in the process it shud not be the case of one wrong replacing another

Kailasa temple in ajanta caves can easily beat down any wonders of the world hand down but 99 pc of well informed people are unaware of its existence

label_values.value: Sir the problem of the common man is that while the Hon'ble judges are all fire and brimstone during the hearing when it comes to passing orders the orders are whatever is demanded by Sibal. U r saying that we shud not blame them bcoz they are backing up the cbi but what about the state govt and Kolkata police attempts to scuttle thr probe the court has been absolutely silent in their orders on the this aspect. And since SC has taken suo motto cognizance and then turned it into a doctor's safety issue and trying to scuttle the protest toeing the line of wd govt and its lawyers. And this is not the only case wherever Calcutta HC catches the govt by its collar SC comes to the rescue with a stay order and then t p t (tarikh pe tarikh) begins. Calcutta HC was doing a fantastic job then what was the need for this suo motto cognizance. Also what steps has the SC taken in their orders to scuttle the investigation.

label_values.value:
I remember in 2016 the Posta under construction flyover had fallen and every Kolkatans heart was aching due to the enormity of the man made tragedy.

During the same time India was playing the semi final of the T20 world cup and the final was to happen in eden gardens. It was the only time in my life that there was a sort of relief when India lost the semi final for it wud hv been really difficult to cheer for India in the aftermath of such a humungous tragedy.

As the citizen of a nation or state or city or society we share a collective conscience which at times demands that we put aside our personal joys and revellries aside and adhere to call of this common conscience to be the sufferers of the tragedy whom we may know but being human shud be above all.

label_values.value: I always wonder what gives some people the right what is class and what is crass - i mean what are the parameters or is there some international class standards board (akin the international accounting standard board). The same things over which these people go (lady) gaga abroad becomes crass when done in India and why shud be copy the west or the British who decides that fork shud be in the left hand and not in the right hand and which part of the wine glass to hold and how to sip it and if it's so why there are no such standards for lassi except that it should create a temporary white moustache. It's high time we overcame this colonial mindsets and thank u Ambanis for showing India that we do things our way and make the who is who of the world wear kurta pajama and saree and make Justin beiber dance to Desi bhangra tunes. Not fair sir zara was setting up factories here icons are setting up their 3rd steel plant without anyone knowing about the 1st two.  People here want to gloat with fiction and nostalgia (so many nostalgic posts about thin arrawrot biscuits).

Market economy requires efficient deployment of capital and resources and capital will move to more efficient venues from less efficent ones. 

The recent press conference of the CM was a huge indicator of the deep rot the state is into where a CM clearly admits about minister taking money to deploy hawkers - I wonder how much lower will we sink to.

Getting the core right 1st is the key

label_values.value:
The day the  people of our state show the same level of distaste/disdain for political violence no political party will dare to indulge in any pre/during/post election violence.
In a democracy, the problem is not with the parties but with the electorate which elects them.

And i dont buy the argument. that all elections are rigged. Local bodies elections may be completely rigged bcoz of the complicity of the state election commission but the outcome of assembly and parliament election reflects people's opinion.

Thankfully my opinion of him has remained same for may be a decade or more. A person with high credentials but very poor/lowly conduct.

We are from a country where Ram is worshipped bcoz of the way he conducted himself no matter what life threw at him.

In case of Amartya babu it's opposite his conduct remains lowly no matter how much life gives him. From illegal land to being only bharat ratna awardee to claim free air India tkts to the poor conduct bordering on corruption  as Head of Nalanda University to his utterly biased and prejudiced utterances against Modi to turning a blind eye to happening in his own state the conduct has been absolutely lowly totally unbecoming of the stature life bestowed upon him.

Calibre and conduct are different things. Ram became bhagwan ram by the way he conducted himself. Arjun was best warrior of his time but his conduct at beginning of the war was lowly and totally unbecoming of a kshatriya, krishna had to narrate entire bhagwad geeta to bring him out of his despondency (and resultant lowly conduct). In spite of all the great qualities Karna had his conduct during draupadi's disrobing incident and at the time of Abhimanyu's killing put a indelible blot on all his greatness. 

Coming to Mr Sen his calibre his intellect, his achievements, his pedigree - all are of the highest order but the way he has conducted himself in spite of being a public person - from claiming free tkts from air India to his highly questionable dealings as chancellor of nalanda university to his blatant bias against the modi govt and at the same time being completely silent on all the wrongdoings right under his nose (some of which u hv mentioned in yr above post) - he will hv the same fate as karna - high on caliber but low on conduct. He is not even close to Arjun and is light years away from Ram.

U took a quote too literally but out of my reverence I still chose to ignore but u asked for a reply so I had to revert. We are essentially saying the same thing neither atiq was a bali nor the killers Ram.

Coming to the moot point about right or wrong I hv already said a few times the battle of adharma cannot be fought thru dharma. The likes of Atiq would hv used the very law against the state and the milords wud grant bail with sermons every sinner has a future and also attach conditions that he shud be released nt 6 pm (refer the Zubair bail case) while the same SC would admonish Nupur Sharma just bcoz she asked for relief from multiple cases being filed against her across the country.

Today Zubair roams freely while nupur sharma will never feel safe for rest of her life after seeing what they did to Salman rushdie after decades.

Thank you. 
"It does add a touch of greatness" therein lies the problem, this sense of regionalism, of a separate identity over above the national identity is a problem our state suffers and even the best of people hv this as u just demonstrated. This is exactly the problem the Gandhi parivar also suffers from - a sense of entitlement bcoz of their ancestry. In my view no matter how many luminaries walked on this soil it adds zero credibility or greatness to the other folks from the state, each has to fight their own battle and prove their own mettle. If u r so happy basking in the glory of this luminaries what incentive does one really hv to make ones own life worthwhile. The sooner the people can come out of this false sense of pride, entitlement, greatness they hv bestowed upon themselves and realise that the last icon the state produced was in 2000 when sourav ganguly became captain and that some of their so called icons - the economists nobelists hv feet of clay - and that after 75 years u cannot keep basking in the glory of freedom fighters - the better it will be for the state and it's citizenry.

I can never overlook the fact that within a space of 11 years from 1980 to 1991 all the 3 main members of family died unnatural and all the power was usurped by one who continues to wield it till date and all the potential challengers be it madhawrao Scindia or rajesh pilot or ysr met the same fate and it also explains why the likes of mms and pranab babu chose survival over bravado.

This forgive on one hand and protest on other hand is just political drama of the type we saw in the central hall of parliament in 2004 to cast the image of tyag ki devi and now it's forgiveness ki devi 

Unfortunately both left and congress do not realise that it is the age of social media these drama won't cut ice with people who don't hv thr patience to watch 5 seconds ad on youtube

Neither buddhababu nor didi did it. It is the people of the state who did it and even today it is next to impossible for someone to set up an industry in the state. The moment u even think about shilpo few netas will come aamar lokder chakri dite hobe. 

This love for chakri, pfer taka, penshaan, chutti etc in lieu of mere phsyical presence has done incalculable harm  to the state and nothing has changed on the ground to undo this.

In our state the sole purpose of an entrepreneur has to be to provide jobs to people who will form unions, align with netas to blackmail the promoter, restrict productivity, indulge in militant trade unionism and destroy the very organisation that feeds them.

Unfortunate but true.

The entire M existence is based on having a distinct visible identity starting from the birth to clothes to topi to beard to name (reason why conversion leads to name change) to food (halal) to burqa to hizab the whole idea is to create a distinct identity and use violence to enforce it 

In the book skin in the game naseem taleb using the exmaple of halal (not halala) has beautifully described how a small minority (2 or 3 pc) can use rigidity to dictate terms to the larger majority making them follow their rules. 

https://medium.com/incerto/the-most-intolerant-wins-the-dictatorship-of-the-small-minority-3f1f83ce4e15

label_values.label: Text
label_values.value:
Pls understand my single point issue is with the rank bad choices people of our state have been making for the last 45 years and are most likely do the same whenever they will get a chance next. My 2nd issue is my inability to understand that iff all are seemingly against the regime then who votes them into power. My sense/hypothesis is that in social gatherings and media people take the politically correct stand (of opposing the regime) but when it comes to exercising their franchise they vote for the very same regime they otherwise seemingly oppose to maintain their intellectual tag.

I m not into any school except that i want to see our state progress for which a progressive govt is required which is becoming more and more unlikely given the demographic situation Bengal has got into. The M vote plus the secular/intellectual vote will be more then 50% and Bengal will continue to suffer.

Vedanta group had one investment in Bengal - India foils ltd with two plants one in kamarhati and one in hoera. They had to sell it to ess Dee aluminium in 2008 and in 2017 the owner of ess Dee sudip dutta was arrested ostensibly for an alleged PF default but actually due to political considerations. The group is now under insolvency.

Such being the state of affairs in the state who in his right mind would even consider investing here and then be made to stand like beggars in investment summits and asked to sign MOUs so that state can make tall claims both sides knowing fully well that not a rupee will actually get invested.

label_values.value:
You can either let yr 5 senses control you or let the Krishna/parmatma/the inner voice guide u. One needs to prepare his/her mind and body in a manner that they become subservient to the inner voice. The inner voice will always guide u towards choosing the right (sattva) over the comfortable (rajas) and over the wrong (Tamas). Its yr body and mind which will overrule the inner voice and make u chose the comfortable/easy/wrong over the rigjt/difficult/tedious. This is an ongoing dharamyudh (war) at kurukshetra (the human being) where arjun (jivatma) has to constantly try listening to Krishna (parmatma) and rather than fall for sensual or mental gratification..

This is my 2 cents from what I read in bhagwad geeta. Thank u for asking such a nice Q. ððð

label_values.value:
Purely from the financial world can say that some decisive steps have been taken to attack corruption at root level 

1. Gst implementation 
2. Companies act compliances are fully online 
3. More than 2 lacs comapnies have been removed and a host of directors have been barred from becoming directors due to non compliance
4. Faceless assessment of income tax is revolutionary
5. UPI has made India the topmost country as far as payment banking is concerned. ERUPI is a continuation of that and also one nation one ration

There have been some implementation issues like the current IT portal, GSTN portal initially, roc portal but with ear to ground i can say tax evasion has gone down and focus on compliance has increased (at the cost of ease of doing business). But in my humble opinion these steps are effective rather than passing another law to deter corruption. Digital is the biggest tool we have to weed out corruption.

label_values.value: I wud surely agree with u if things were as simple as u make them out to be. If u can vouch that these pharma companies do not indulge in worst of inhuman practices and their cupboard is full of skeletons i will accept that their profits is legitimate. Right now there is a clear battle going on btwn pfizer and govt of India where pfizer wants access to Indian market for their vaccine (at any cost) but also wants immunity from all kinds of visceral liabilities. U can say these are legitimate business practices for increasing shareholder value but then India is not even asking for their vaccine and kind of games that are being played in the background illegitimately manipulating the media ang the local politicians and creating a vaccination hesitancy regarding other vaccines - if all these are legitimate means of earning profits then probably we hv different definitions got legitimacy and ethics.

Sir my views are quite strong and contrarian on this matter. Even if the entire world finds communism most irrelevant Bengal will continue to find it relevant on some pretext or other and even when they remove the left they go for ultra left (tmc) and then reelect them and even now there seems to be a decent probability of them giving tmc another chance. 

Coming to left - a bunch of people who wud support China in 1962 war, wud make a tukde tukde gang member kanhaiya Kumar their leader, aishe ghosh another lumpen element is now being promoted. Can't think of one good deed that the communists hv done for the country except closure of industries, taking sides of anti national forces, always opposing anything that's good for the country, supporting economically disastrous policies and indulging in most inhuman and gruesome acts like sainbari and still Bengal thinks the left and their left 2.0 (tmc) is relevant. 

No wonder Bengal continues to be in a mess and will get messier if people repeat the same foolish mistake they hv been making for the last 4 decades.

My apologies for my rather radical tone but having made a choice to stay put in Bengal hv suffered lack of growth and opportunities like so many others.

RRR has anyway shown himself as a stooge of congress by his subsequent events.

label_values.value:
Some overriding Qs

1) will the people of the state be sensible enough to vote out a govt which has been a worse extension of the regime they voted out in 2011. 

2) Will the people of Bengal finally learn to make sensible and practical political choices and get Bengal into mainstream rather than being a rebel without a cause it has been since the 40/50 years. 

3) Will Bengal become free from political violence like rest of India (forget prosecution not even arrest happens for a political murder in Bengal both parties blame each other and the matter ends). 

There is a lot Bengal needs to think today what rest of the world/india has already thought and done.

Bengal - if thou shall do it (in 2021) thou shall grow
if not fates with rebels (without a cause) do conspire. 
(due apologies to Shakespeare)

Why is it that some incidents become more dastardly than others. Every such incident is as deplorable and the perpetrator as culpable as nirbhaya or hathras but for the media incl social media such crimes hv to meet certain criteria of caste, class and most importantly the state in which the incident occurred. Why shud 
Nirbhaya/hathras/kamduni deserve so much coverage but something equally heinous in dinajpur (West Bengal) is conveniently forgotten/ignored.

Those who end up googling for dinajpur (happened in Jan 2020) are a testimony to what I hv said above.
